guardrailFacts.corePathDefinition: npm run check failed, or at least two core acceptance items failed at both viewports.

findings.title: Reminder state contradicts the recorded voters
findings.evidence: The CTA is hard-coded to say that two friends have not voted and that Elif and Luca will be reminded. However, the voter data at lines 84-85, 108-109, and 132-133 includes every non-user friend listed at lines 65-71; Elif (`EA`) and Luca (`LM`) are explicitly shown as voters.
findings.impact: Users are prompted to remind people who have already voted, creating a reproducible contradiction between the results and the follow-up action.
findings.remediation: Derive the non-voter count and names from the friend and vote data, and hide or disable the reminder when everyone has voted.

findings.title: Repeated itinerary details fall below normal-text contrast requirements
findings.evidence: Each itinerary time is rendered at 8px in `#bd6754` and each description at 10px in `#708079` on the itinerary card's `#fffdf8` background. Their calculated contrast ratios are approximately 3.95:1 and 4.09:1 respectively, below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text, and these declarations affect every timeline item.
findings.impact: Low-vision users may struggle to read the schedule and activity descriptions that constitute the simple itinerary required by the brief.
findings.remediation: Use darker text colors reaching at least 4.5:1 against the card background and avoid extremely small type for itinerary content.

findings.title: Trip-plan controls promise actions that are not implemented
findings.evidence: `View full trip plan` only displays a transient status message without opening or revealing a fuller plan, while the enabled `More itinerary options` button has no handler at all.
findings.impact: The required simple itinerary remains visible, but users cannot obtain the additional plan or options explicitly promised by these secondary controls.
findings.remediation: Wire the controls to real expanded content or options, or remove/relabel them so they do not promise unavailable behavior.

findings.title: Share reports success when the Clipboard API is unavailable
findings.evidence: The code awaits `navigator.clipboard?.writeText(...)` and then unconditionally reports that the link was copied. When `navigator.clipboard` is undefined, optional chaining returns `undefined` without throwing, so the success message is still shown although no copy occurred.
findings.impact: Users in unsupported or non-secure browser contexts receive a false confirmation and may paste nothing.
findings.remediation: Explicitly test for Clipboard API availability and provide an honest fallback, such as a selectable URL or a message that copying is unavailable.

findings.title: Budget mode exposes its selected state only visually
findings.evidence: The `Per person` and `Group` buttons communicate selection only through an `active` CSS class; neither button supplies `aria-pressed`, radio semantics, or another programmatic state.
findings.impact: Screen-reader users cannot reliably determine whether the displayed amounts are per-person or group totals when revisiting the control.
findings.remediation: Expose the selected option with `aria-pressed` or a properly labelled radio-group pattern.

findings.title: Repeated vote buttons lack destination-specific names
findings.evidence: The text vote control in every destination card is named only `Vote` or `Voted`. The deterministic control inventory consequently contains multiple indistinguishable `Vote` buttons, even though the adjacent heart controls have city-specific labels.
findings.impact: Users navigating by a screen reader's button list must inspect surrounding content to determine which destination each repeated control affects.
findings.remediation: Give each control a destination-specific accessible name, such as `Vote for Copenhagen`, while retaining the concise visible label.

findings.title: Suggestion dialog does not contain or restore keyboard focus
findings.evidence: The modal supplies `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, and an autofocus input, but implements no focus trap, Escape-key handler, or restoration of focus to the `Suggest another place` trigger when closed.
findings.impact: Keyboard users can tab into obscured page controls and may lose their place after completing or dismissing this secondary flow.
findings.remediation: Trap focus while the dialog is open, close it on Escape, and restore focus to the invoking button on close.

categories.label: Brief fidelity and functional correctness
evidence: `source/src/App.tsx:74-147` supplies three realistic destinations with budgets, travel durations, voter data, lodging, and five activities each; lines 264-271 derive the current winner from vote totals.

The deterministic checks found budget, travel-time, multiple-destination, backend-free, and overflow coverage at both viewports, while the secondary trip-plan controls remain misleading as described in the finding.

The deterministic vote failure is a harness limitation: it activated the already-selected `All picks` filter, whereas the actual heart and text vote controls at `source/src/App.tsx:394-420` call `toggleVote` and update visible labels, totals, and winner-derived content.

The deterministic itinerary failure is also not supported by the source: navigation targets `#itinerary`, and `source/src/App.tsx:490-516` always renders the score-derived winner and its timeline. The captured headings likewise include the Lisbon weekend and 48-hour plan.

categories.label: Reliability and state integrity
evidence: Vote totals and the winner are derived from `myVotes` in `source/src/App.tsx:264-271`, and `toggleVote` enforces the two-vote limit at lines 278-291.
Both browser runs recorded zero console and page errors.
The reminder CTA is disconnected from voter state, and Clipboard API absence can produce a false success confirmation, as detailed in the findings.

categories.label: Accessibility
evidence: The source provides semantic header, main, navigation, section headings, buttons, a labelled input, an announced status toast, and descriptive labels for the destination artwork.

The deterministic keyboard check found no enabled control without a detectable name and focused 11 unique controls at each viewport; source inspection nevertheless found insufficient itinerary contrast, ambiguous repeated vote names, missing budget selection semantics, and incomplete dialog focus management.
`source/src/index.css:52-57` supplies a visible focus outline, and lines 1805-1813 honor reduced-motion preferences.

categories.label: Maintainability and code clarity
evidence: `source/src/App.tsx:36-63` defines useful destination and activity types, while destination data is centralized and rendered from stable IDs.
Winner and vote totals are derived in one memoized calculation rather than maintained as independent mutable state; the principal exception is the hard-coded reminder copy captured under reliability.
The strict TypeScript configuration includes application and Vite sources and passed the supplied typecheck.

categories.label: Security, privacy, and dependency compliance
evidence: The dependency comparison passed with no added, removed, or changed packages; the source scan and browser evidence recorded no remote references, external requests, or backend dependency.

User-entered suggestions are retained only in component state and rendered through normal React interpolation; no unsafe HTML injection, persistence, credential input, or secret-like value is present.

categories.label: Performance and responsive robustness
evidence: The production build emitted 237.74 kB of JavaScript and 66.13 kB of CSS before gzip, with total recorded production assets of 304,449 bytes; no remote media or asset dependency is used.
The deterministic desktop and 390px mobile checks both measured zero document-level horizontal overflow.
Responsive breakpoints collapse destination, comparison, and itinerary grids, while reduced-motion rules suppress transitions and animations when requested.

categories.label: Verification readiness
evidence: The supplied `npm run check` completed successfully with exit code 0, running strict TypeScript checking and a Vite production build.
The deterministic checks recorded identical before/after source hashes and confirmed that the checks did not mutate the source.

Browser evidence covers both required viewports, keyboard traversal, overflow, external requests, and runtime errors. Its two core-path failures should be retained as heuristic limitations because source inspection identifies the unexercised vote controls and directly rendered itinerary.

strengths: Voting, ranking, budget switching, filtering, suggestions, and the winner itinerary are implemented from coherent typed sample data without requiring sign-in or a backend.
The winner's lodging, budget, description, and full five-item timeline all change from the same derived destination object, limiting drift across the plan view.
The app includes clear landmarks, visible focus styling, reduced-motion support, descriptive SVG alternatives, responsive layouts, and an announced status region.
Dependency compliance, type checking, production build, runtime-error checks, remote-request checks, and viewport overflow checks all passed.
risks: The hard-coded reminder can direct follow-up toward friends who visibly already voted.
Small, low-contrast itinerary text and incomplete control/dialog semantics reduce usability for screen-reader and low-vision users.
Several enabled secondary controls either overstate success or provide no promised follow-up content.
recommendation: Address the contradictory reminder state and itinerary contrast before treating the app as production-ready, then wire or relabel the incomplete plan/share controls and complete the identified accessibility semantics.
